items.title: Skills for Claude Code and Codex
items.text:
This repo gives you a reusable sub-agent workflow for two tools:

  • Claude Code
  • Codex

The short version is simple:

  1. Install the skills from this repo.
  2. Run one setup script.
  3. Start working in your tool or target project.

items.title: Choose Your Path
items.text:
• Quick Start for the shortest setup steps
  • How to Use These Skills for the longer explanation
  • FAQ for common setup questions

items.title: What Is In This Repo
items.text:
• orchestrator-workflow/ for complex implementation tasks
  • refactor-workflow/ for refactoring tasks
  • claude-config/ for Claude-specific global config
  • codex/AGENTS.md for Codex project instructions
  • scripts/ for install helpers

items.title: When To Use This Repo
items.text:
Use this repo when:

  • you want the same orchestration style in Claude Code
  • you want Codex to work with a project-level AGENTS.md
  • you want a shared workflow layer that target projects can extend locally

items.title: Start Here
items.text: If you only need the practical steps, go to Quick Start.

items.title: Do I need both Claude and Codex?
items.text:
No. You can use only one path.

  • If you use Claude Code, install the skills and run ./scripts/install-claude-config.sh.
  • If you use Codex, install the skills and run ./scripts/install-codex-agents.sh /path/to/your/project.

items.title: Why are skills and Claude config separate steps?
items.text:
Because they do different jobs.

  • the skills add reusable workflow logic
  • the Claude config installs Claude-specific files into ~/.claude

For Claude, you usually want both.

items.title: Does Codex need claude-config/?
items.text:
No.

For Codex, the important pieces are:

  • the installed skills
  • AGENTS.md inside the target project

items.title: Do I run the scripts from my target project?
items.text:
No.

Clone this repo and run the scripts from this repo root.

Example:

cd /path/to/skills
./scripts/install-claude-config.sh
./scripts/install-codex-agents.sh /path/to/your/project

items.title: What does install-codex-agents.sh actually do?
items.text:
It copies the repository file codex/AGENTS.md into your target repo as AGENTS.md.

That file tells Codex how to use this orchestration workflow.

items.title: Can my target project still have its own local rules?
items.text:
Yes.

This repo is the shared orchestration layer. Your target project can still define its own local roles and rules in AGENTS.md or .agents/**/SKILL.md.

items.title: Quick Start
items.text: Clone this repo first. Run the commands below from the repo root.

items.title: Claude
items.text:
1. Install Claude Code.
  2. Install the skills from this repo:
npx skills add <your-git-url-or-owner/repo> --skill '*' -g -a claude-code -y
  1. Run:
./scripts/install-claude-config.sh

Done:

  • Claude Code gets the reusable skills
  • the Claude config is copied into ~/.claude

items.title: Codex
items.text:
1. Install Codex.
  2. Install the skills from this repo:
npx skills add <your-git-url-or-owner/repo> --skill '*' -g -a codex -y
  1. Run:
./scripts/install-codex-agents.sh /path/to/your/project

Done:

  • Codex gets the reusable skills
  • your target repo gets an AGENTS.md file for this workflow

items.title: If You Use Both
items.text:
Install both skill targets at once:

npx skills add <your-git-url-or-owner/repo> --skill '*' -g -a claude-code -a codex -y

Then run:

./scripts/install-claude-config.sh
./scripts/install-codex-agents.sh /path/to/your/project

Use this when:

  • you want Claude to get the global config from claude-config/
  • you want Codex to get AGENTS.md inside a working project

items.title: What The Scripts Do
items.text:
./scripts/install-claude-config.sh

  • creates ~/.claude
  • copies CLAUDE.md
  • copies agents/, commands/, and output-styles/
  • installs statusline-command.sh

./scripts/install-codex-agents.sh /path/to/your/project

  • creates the target directory if needed
  • copies codex/AGENTS.md into that repo as AGENTS.md

items.title: How to Use These Skills
items.text:
This repo is meant to stay small and reusable.

It gives you:

  • shared orchestration skills
  • Claude-specific global config
  • Codex project instructions

items.title: The Main Idea
items.text:
There are two layers:

  1. Shared skills that both Claude Code and Codex can use
  2. Tool-specific setup for the tool you actually work with

That is why setup happens in two steps instead of one.

items.title: Claude Flow
items.text:
Claude uses:

  • installed skills from this repo
  • files copied into ~/.claude by ./scripts/install-claude-config.sh

This gives Claude the reusable workflow plus the Claude-specific global setup.

items.title: Codex Flow
items.text:
Codex uses:

  • installed skills from this repo
  • AGENTS.md inside the target repo

This gives Codex the reusable workflow plus project-level instructions.

items.title: How To Adapt This To Your Own Project
items.text:
Keep this repo focused on orchestration.

Put stack-specific roles in the target project, for example:

  • AGENTS.md
  • .agents/**/SKILL.md
  • project-specific rules files

The target repo should describe its own stack and domain. This repo should stay generic.

items.title: Recommended Reading Order
items.text:
If you are new to this repo:

  1. Read Quick Start
  2. Use the Claude or Codex path
  3. Come back here only if you need the longer explanation
